summary.intent: Fix Slack socket mode being completely broken in moltis due to a TLS configuration issue preventing WebSocket connections to wss://wss-primary.slack.com.
summary.outcome: Bumped slack-morphism from 2.18.0 to 2.20.0 in workspace Cargo.toml, which fixed the upstream bug where the rustls-native-certs feature was missing the tokio-tungstenite/rustls-tls-native-roots dependency. All 70 slack tests pass and the crate compiles cleanly.

repo: The workspace Cargo.toml uses a single slack-morphism entry with features = ["axum", "hyper"] and a semver constraint; bumping the constraint there is the canonical way to update it across the codebase.

The workspace has two separate tokio-tungstenite versions in play: 0.26 (used by providers, httpd, node-host via workspace dep) and 0.28/0.29 (pulled in transitively by slack-morphism) — these resolve independently due to semver incompatibility.
slack-morphism API usage in crates/slack/ is entirely through slack_morphism::prelude::* glob imports, making major-version-compatible upgrades low-risk.
The project uses a 'beads' issue tracker (not GitHub Issues directly) with a claim/close workflow integrated into the development process.

code.finding: slack-morphism is declared here as a crate-level dep that references the workspace entry
Workspace-level slack-morphism entry changed from version '2.6' (resolved 2.18.0) to '2.20', fixing the TLS feature gap
Socket mode implementation uses slack_morphism::prelude::* — stable across 2.x, no API changes needed for the version bump
Outbound Slack messaging also uses prelude glob import only
Webhook handler also uses prelude glob import only

workflow: Cargo feature unification can be used as a workaround when a transitive dep has incorrect feature flags — adding the dep directly with the correct feature forces it onto the unified instance.
cargo metadata --format-version 1 | jq is effective for inspecting resolved features on specific crate versions in the dependency graph.
Checking crates.io API (crates.io/api/v1/crates/<name>/<version>) directly provides the exact feature definitions to compare across versions without needing to check out source.

friction: Multiple bash commands were needed to trace the feature flag chain through slack-morphism → tokio-tungstenite → tungstenite before the root cause became clear.

The initial plan required a workaround (adding tokio-tungstenite directly with the correct feature); this was invalidated once version 2.20.0 was found to fix the upstream bug, requiring a pivot mid-analysis.
WebFetch via ToolSearch was needed to inspect crates.io API because the Bash tool alone couldn't easily retrieve structured crate metadata.
open_items: The upstream slack-morphism bug (missing rustls-tls-native-roots in rustls-native-certs feature) was identified but it's unclear if an upstream issue was filed — this was mentioned as a should-do but not confirmed as done.
tokio-tungstenite bumped from 0.28.0 to 0.29.0 as a side effect of the slack-morphism upgrade — no compatibility issues were found but this was not deeply audited.
The workspace tokio-tungstenite 0.26 dep (used by providers, httpd, node-host) is now diverged further from the slack path's 0.29 — eventual consolidation may be worth tracking.
